Title: Capture a valid height in centimeters and save
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has an active visit; on the Capture Vitals form
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient dashboard | 2. Click Capture Vitals | 3. Enter Height value | 4. Confirm unit is cm | 5. Click Save
Expected_Result: Height observation is saved and displayed as 170 cm on the Vitals dashboard with the current date/time

Title: Capture a valid weight in kilograms and save
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has an active visit; on the Capture Vitals form
Test_Steps: 1. Open Capture Vitals | 2. Enter Weight value | 3. Confirm unit is kg | 4. Click Save
Expected_Result: Weight observation is saved and shown as 68.5 kg on the Vitals dashboard

Title: Capture systolic and diastolic blood pressure and save
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has an active visit; on the Capture Vitals form
Test_Steps: 1. Open Capture Vitals | 2. Enter Systolic value | 3. Enter Diastolic value | 4. Click Save
Expected_Result: BP is saved and displayed as 120/80 mmHg on the Vitals dashboard

Title: Capture a valid pulse rate and save
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has an active visit; on the Capture Vitals form
Test_Steps: 1. Open Capture Vitals | 2. Enter Pulse value | 3. Click Save
Expected_Result: Pulse observation is saved and displayed as 72 beats/min

Title: Capture body temperature in Celsius and save
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has an active visit; on the Capture Vitals form
Test_Steps: 1. Open Capture Vitals | 2. Enter Temperature value | 3. Confirm unit is C | 4. Click Save
Expected_Result: Temperature observation is saved and shown as 36.8 C on the Vitals dashboard

Title: Capture oxygen saturation (SpO2) and save
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has an active visit; on the Capture Vitals form
Test_Steps: 1. Open Capture Vitals | 2. Enter SpO2 value | 3. Click Save
Expected_Result: SpO2 observation is saved and displayed as 98% on the Vitals dashboard

Title: Capture respiratory rate and save
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has an active visit; on the Capture Vitals form
Test_Steps: 1. Open Capture Vitals | 2. Enter Respiratory Rate value | 3. Click Save
Expected_Result: Respiratory rate is saved and displayed as 16 breaths/min

Title: Capture a full set of vitals in a single submission
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has an active visit; on the Capture Vitals form
Test_Steps: 1. Open Capture Vitals | 2. Enter Height | 3. Enter Weight | 4. Enter Systolic and Diastolic | 5. Enter Pulse | 6. Enter Temperature | 7. Enter SpO2 | 8. Enter Resp Rate | 9. Click Save
Test_Data: Height=175; Weight=70; BP=118/76; Pulse=68; Temp=37.0; SpO2=99; Resp=14
Expected_Result: All entered vitals are persisted in one encounter and rendered together on the Vitals dashboard

Title: Verify BMI is auto-calculated from height and weight
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has an active visit; on the Capture Vitals form
Test_Steps: 1. Open Capture Vitals | 2. Enter Height | 3. Enter Weight | 4. Click Save | 5. View Vitals dashboard
Expected_Result: BMI is auto-computed and displayed as 23.5 kg/m2 (68 / 1.70^2) without manual entry

Title: Verify BMI recalculates when weight is edited
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; a vitals encounter with height and weight already exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open the existing vitals encounter | 2. Edit the Weight value | 3. Save | 4. View Vitals dashboard
Test_Data: Old Weight=68; New Weight=80; Height=170 cm
Expected_Result: BMI updates from 23.5 to 27.7 kg/m2 reflecting the new weight; transition Saved to Edited is reflected

Title: Reject save when a required BP component is missing
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; on the Capture Vitals form where BP is configured as required-paired
Test_Steps: 1. Open Capture Vitals | 2. Enter Systolic only | 3. Leave Diastolic empty | 4. Click Save
Expected_Result: Form blocks save with a validation message requiring the paired diastolic value; no partial BP is persisted

Title: Save successfully with only optional vitals entered
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; on the Capture Vitals form where all fields are optional
Test_Steps: 1. Open Capture Vitals | 2. Enter Pulse only | 3. Leave all other fields blank | 4. Click Save
Expected_Result: Encounter saves with only the Pulse observation; no error for blank optional fields

Title: Reject non-numeric text in a numeric vital field
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; on the Capture Vitals form
Test_Steps: 1. Open Capture Vitals | 2. Type alphabetic text into Weight | 3. Attempt to Save
Expected_Result: Field rejects non-numeric input or save is blocked with a numeric validation error; no observation stored

Title: Reject negative value for weight
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; on the Capture Vitals form
Test_Steps: 1. Open Capture Vitals | 2. Enter a negative Weight | 3. Attempt to Save
Expected_Result: Save is blocked with an out-of-range/invalid value message; no negative observation persisted

Title: Reject zero for pulse where physiologically invalid
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; on the Capture Vitals form
Test_Steps: 1. Open Capture Vitals | 2. Enter 0 for Pulse | 3. Attempt to Save
Expected_Result: Save is blocked or value flagged invalid per configured absolute range; clinician is warned

Title: Accept height at the lower allowed boundary
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; Height absolute range configured 10 to 272 cm
Test_Steps: 1. Open Capture Vitals | 2. Enter Height at the minimum allowed value | 3. Click Save
Expected_Result: Height of 10 cm is accepted and saved as the valid lower boundary

Title: Reject height just below the lower boundary
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; Height absolute range configured 10 to 272 cm
Test_Steps: 1. Open Capture Vitals | 2. Enter Height just under the minimum | 3. Attempt to Save
Expected_Result: Save is blocked with an out-of-absolute-range error; observation not persisted

Title: Accept height at the upper allowed boundary
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; Height absolute range configured 10 to 272 cm
Test_Steps: 1. Open Capture Vitals | 2. Enter Height at the maximum allowed value | 3. Click Save
Expected_Result: Height of 272 cm is accepted as the valid upper boundary

Title: Reject height just above the upper boundary
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; Height absolute range configured 10 to 272 cm
Test_Steps: 1. Open Capture Vitals | 2. Enter Height just over the maximum | 3. Attempt to Save
Expected_Result: Save is blocked with an out-of-absolute-range error

Title: Accept temperature at the lower critical boundary
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; Temperature absolute range configured 25 to 43 C
Test_Steps: 1. Open Capture Vitals | 2. Enter Temperature at the minimum allowed | 3. Click Save
Expected_Result: Temperature of 25.0 C is accepted at the lower boundary and flagged critically low

Title: Reject temperature above the upper absolute boundary
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; Temperature absolute range configured 25 to 43 C
Test_Steps: 1. Open Capture Vitals | 2. Enter Temperature above the maximum | 3. Attempt to Save
Expected_Result: Save is blocked with an out-of-absolute-range error

Title: Accept SpO2 at the 100 percent upper boundary
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; SpO2 range configured 0 to 100 percent
Test_Steps: 1. Open Capture Vitals | 2. Enter SpO2 at 100 | 3. Click Save
Expected_Result: SpO2 of 100% is accepted as the valid maximum

Title: Reject SpO2 above 100 percent
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; SpO2 range configured 0 to 100 percent
Test_Steps: 1. Open Capture Vitals | 2. Enter SpO2 above 100 | 3. Attempt to Save
Expected_Result: Save is blocked with an out-of-range error; value cannot exceed 100%

Title: Verify decimal precision handling for temperature
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; on the Capture Vitals form
Test_Steps: 1. Open Capture Vitals | 2. Enter Temperature with one decimal place | 3. Click Save | 4. View dashboard
Expected_Result: Decimal value is stored and displayed without rounding loss as 37.5 C

Title: Flag critically high blood pressure on save
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; abnormal/critical ranges configured for BP
Test_Steps: 1. Open Capture Vitals | 2. Enter critically high Systolic | 3. Enter high Diastolic | 4. Save | 5. View dashboard
Expected_Result: BP is saved and visually flagged as critical/high (abnormal indicator) on the Vitals dashboard

Title: Flag low blood pressure (hypotension) on save
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; abnormal ranges configured for BP
Test_Steps: 1. Open Capture Vitals | 2. Enter low Systolic | 3. Enter low Diastolic | 4. Save
Expected_Result: BP is flagged as abnormally low on the dashboard

Title: Normal blood pressure shows no abnormal flag
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; abnormal ranges configured for BP
Test_Steps: 1. Open Capture Vitals | 2. Enter normal Systolic and Diastolic | 3. Save
Expected_Result: BP is saved without any abnormal/critical indicator

Title: Decision table for temperature classification
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; abnormal/critical ranges configured for temperature
Test_Steps: 1. Open Capture Vitals | 2. Enter Temperature for each band | 3. Save | 4. Verify flag per band
Expected_Result: Each temperature maps to the correct classification flag (Critical Low / Low / Normal / High / Critical High)

Title: Decision table for pulse classification
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; abnormal ranges configured for pulse
Test_Steps: 1. Open Capture Vitals | 2. Enter Pulse for each band | 3. Save | 4. Verify flag
Expected_Result: Each pulse value maps to the correct flag (Bradycardia / Normal / Tachycardia / Critical)

Title: Flag critically low SpO2 (hypoxemia)
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; critical SpO2 threshold configured (e.g. below 90)
Test_Steps: 1. Open Capture Vitals | 2. Enter low SpO2 | 3. Save | 4. View dashboard
Expected_Result: SpO2 is saved and flagged as critically low (hypoxemia) on the dashboard

Title: Capture weight in pounds and verify storage normalization
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; unit toggle for weight available (kg/lb)
Test_Steps: 1. Open Capture Vitals | 2. Switch Weight unit to lb | 3. Enter Weight in lb | 4. Save | 5. View stored value
Expected_Result: Weight is correctly converted/normalized (154 lb = 69.85 kg) and displayed consistently with the configured display unit

Title: Capture temperature in Fahrenheit and verify conversion
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; unit toggle for temperature available (C/F)
Test_Steps: 1. Open Capture Vitals | 2. Switch Temperature unit to F | 3. Enter Temperature in F | 4. Save | 5. View stored value
Expected_Result: Temperature is converted/normalized correctly (98.6 F = 37.0 C) and displayed with the configured unit

Title: Verify height unit label is shown next to value
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; a height observation exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open Vitals dashboard | 2. Locate the Height observation
Expected_Result: The unit label cm is displayed adjacent to the height value to avoid ambiguity

Title: Verify vitals are saved under a single encounter with provider and datetime
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; active visit; encounter type Vitals configured
Test_Steps: 1. Open Capture Vitals | 2. Enter several vitals | 3. Save | 4. Open encounter details
Expected_Result: All vitals are grouped in one Vitals encounter stamped with encounter datetime and the entering provider

Title: Verify latest vitals appear on the patient dashboard widget
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has at least one vitals encounter
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient dashboard | 2. Locate the Vitals widget
Expected_Result: The Vitals widget displays the most recent values with their capture timestamp

Title: Verify historical vitals trend/list ordering
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has multiple vitals encounters over time
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient dashboard | 2. Open the Vitals history/trends view
Expected_Result: Vitals are listed in reverse-chronological order with correct dates; oldest does not display as latest

Title: Edit an existing vitals value and verify update
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician with edit privilege; a vitals encounter exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open the vitals encounter | 2. Edit a value | 3. Save | 4. View dashboard
Expected_Result: The observation transitions to the edited value (82) and the previous value is no longer the active reading

Title: Void an erroneous observation with a reason
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician with void privilege; a vitals observation exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open the observation | 2. Select Void/Delete | 3. Enter a void reason | 4. Confirm
Expected_Result: Observation is voided (state Active to Voided), removed from active dashboard view, and retained with reason for audit

Title: Verify void requires a non-empty reason
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician with void privilege; an observation exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open the observation | 2. Select Void | 3. Leave reason blank | 4. Confirm
Expected_Result: Void is blocked with a validation message requiring a reason; observation remains active

Title: Verify voided observation is excluded from BMI recalculation
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; voided weight observation exists with a later valid weight
Test_Steps: 1. Void an earlier weight | 2. View Vitals dashboard | 3. Inspect BMI
Expected_Result: BMI reflects only the active weight (27.7) and ignores the voided value

Title: Pairwise combination of unit selections across vitals
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; unit toggles available for weight/temperature/height
Test_Steps: 1. Open Capture Vitals | 2. Set unit combination per the pairwise set | 3. Enter values | 4. Save | 5. Verify normalized storage
Test_Data: Combo: Weight=lb, Temp=C, Height=cm; and Weight=kg, Temp=F, Height=in
Expected_Result: Each unit combination saves and normalizes correctly with no cross-field interference

Title: Pairwise of user role and visit state for capture access
Preconditions: Test users for Clinician, Nurse, Receptionist roles; patients in active and no-active-visit states
Test_Steps: 1. Log in per role | 2. Attempt Capture Vitals for each visit state | 3. Record access outcome
Expected_Result: Clinician/Nurse can capture during an active visit; Receptionist is denied; no-active-visit prompts to start a visit

Title: Deny vitals capture to a user lacking the privilege
Preconditions: User with a role that has no Capture Vitals / Edit Observations privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Log in as the unprivileged user | 2. Navigate to the patient | 3. Attempt to open Capture Vitals
Expected_Result: Access is denied or the Capture Vitals action is not available; no vitals can be created

Title: Verify edit/void actions are recorded with user and timestamp
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician with edit/void privileges; auditing enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Edit a vitals value | 2. Void another observation | 3. Inspect audit/history log
Expected_Result: Audit log captures who changed/voided what and when, supporting accountability and HIPAA traceability

Title: Reject script/SQL-like input in a free-text vitals note
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; vitals form has a comment/note field
Test_Steps: 1. Open Capture Vitals | 2. Enter a script/SQL payload in the note | 3. Save | 4. View dashboard
Expected_Result: Input is sanitized/escaped; no script executes and no injection occurs; value stored as inert text

Title: Verify vitals data is not exposed without authentication
Preconditions: Application requires login; a patient with vitals exists
Test_Steps: 1. Log out | 2. Attempt to access the vitals page/API URL directly
Expected_Result: Access is denied/redirected to login; no PHI vitals are returned to an unauthenticated request

Title: Create a vitals encounter via the REST API
Preconditions: Valid API session/token for a Clinician; patient and visit UUIDs known
Test_Steps: 1. POST to the encounter endpoint with a vitals obs group | 2. Capture response | 3. GET the encounter
Test_Data: Concept: Pulse=72; Patient UUID; Encounter type=Vitals
Expected_Result: API returns 201/200 with the created encounter UUID; obs values match the request

Title: Reject API vitals create with out-of-range value
Preconditions: Valid API session for a Clinician; absolute range configured
Test_Steps: 1. POST a vitals obs with an out-of-absolute-range value | 2. Inspect response
Expected_Result: API responds with a 4xx validation error and does not persist the observation

Title: Reject unauthenticated API request for vitals
Preconditions: No valid token; vitals endpoint reachable
Test_Steps: 1. Send a GET to the vitals/obs endpoint with no credentials
Expected_Result: API returns 401 Unauthorized; no PHI is disclosed

Title: Retrieve a vitals Observation resource and validate vital-signs category
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint available; a vitals Observation exists for the patient
Test_Steps: 1. GET Observation by patient and category vital-signs | 2. Inspect the returned resource
Expected_Result: Observation has category vital-signs, a valid LOINC code, valueQuantity with UCUM unit, and effectiveDateTime

Title: Verify correct LOINC codes for individual vital signs
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint; vitals Observations exist
Test_Steps: 1. GET each vital Observation | 2. Check the code.coding LOINC value
Test_Data: Body weight=29463-7; Body height=8302-2; Heart rate=8867-4; SpO2=59408-5
Expected_Result: Each vital uses its correct LOINC code and the http://loinc.org system URI

Title: Verify blood pressure is represented as components under LOINC 85354-9
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint; a BP Observation exists
Test_Steps: 1. GET the blood pressure Observation | 2. Inspect components
Expected_Result: BP Observation has code 85354-9 with two components (systolic 8480-6, diastolic 8462-4), each with mmHg UCUM units

Title: Verify UCUM units on vital-sign valueQuantity
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint; vitals Observations exist
Test_Steps: 1. GET vitals Observations | 2. Check valueQuantity.system and code
Test_Data: Weight unit=kg; Temp unit=Cel; SpO2 unit=%
Expected_Result: valueQuantity.system is http://unitsofmeasure.org and codes use valid UCUM (kg, Cel, %)

Title: Verify inbound HL7 v2 ORU vital result maps to an observation
Preconditions: HL7 v2 interface configured; adapter ingests ORU R01 messages
Test_Steps: 1. Send an ORU^R01 with OBX vital-sign segments | 2. Process the message | 3. Verify the created observation
Test_Data: OBX|1|NM|8867-4^Heart rate^LN||72|/min; PID with patient identifier
Expected_Result: ORU message is parsed; OBX maps to the correct vital observation with value 72 and unit; patient matched via PID

Title: Verify HL7 ORU abnormal flag (OBX-8) is honored
Preconditions: HL7 v2 interface configured; abnormal flag mapping enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Send an ORU with OBX-8 set to H (high) | 2. Process | 3. Inspect the stored observation
Expected_Result: Imported observation is marked abnormal/high consistent with OBX-8 = H

Title: Verify a saved numeric obs is stored with concept, value, and units in the obs table
Preconditions: DB read access to the test environment; a vitals encounter was just saved
Test_Steps: 1. Save a pulse via UI | 2. Query the obs table for the encounter | 3. Inspect the row
Expected_Result: obs row exists with the Pulse concept_id, value_numeric=72, correct encounter_id, and voided=0

Title: Verify voiding sets voided flag and reason without hard delete
Preconditions: DB read access; an observation was voided via UI
Test_Steps: 1. Void an observation in the UI | 2. Query the obs row
Expected_Result: obs row has voided=1, voided_by, date_voided, and void_reason populated; the row is not physically deleted

Title: Verify decimal vital values are stored without truncation in the database
Preconditions: DB read access; a decimal temperature was saved
Test_Steps: 1. Save Temperature=37.5 | 2. Query value_numeric for the obs
Expected_Result: value_numeric stores 37.5 with no truncation or rounding error

Title: Verify vitals form fields have accessible labels for screen readers
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; screen reader/axe tooling available
Test_Steps: 1. Open Capture Vitals | 2. Inspect each input for an associated label | 3. Tab through fields with a screen reader
Test_Data: Fields: Height, Weight, BP, Pulse, Temp, SpO2
Expected_Result: Every input has a programmatic label/aria-label; screen reader announces field name and unit

Title: Verify the vitals form is fully operable by keyboard
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; on the Capture Vitals form
Test_Steps: 1. Tab through all fields in logical order | 2. Enter values via keyboard | 3. Activate Save with Enter/Space
Expected_Result: All fields are reachable in a logical tab order and the form can be completed and saved without a mouse

Title: Verify abnormal flag is conveyed beyond color alone
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; an abnormal vital is displayed
Test_Steps: 1. Display an abnormal/critical vital | 2. Inspect the indicator | 3. Check contrast and non-color cue
Expected_Result: Abnormal status uses an icon/text label in addition to color and meets WCAG contrast, so color-blind users are not excluded

Title: Explore concurrent edits to the same vitals encounter by two users
Preconditions: Two Clinician sessions open the same vitals encounter simultaneously
Test_Steps: 1. User A edits Pulse | 2. User B edits the same Pulse | 3. Both save in sequence | 4. Inspect final state and history
Test_Data: User A Pulse=80; User B Pulse=90
Expected_Result: System handles the conflict deterministically (last-write-wins or conflict warning) without data corruption; history reflects both actions

Title: Explore decimal separator and locale handling on input
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; browser locale set to a comma-decimal locale
Test_Steps: 1. Switch browser/app locale | 2. Enter a decimal vital using locale separator | 3. Save | 4. Verify stored value
Expected_Result: Value is interpreted correctly as 37.5 (or input is clearly validated); no silent misinterpretation as 375

Title: Explore rejection of a future-dated vitals encounter datetime
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; encounter datetime editable
Test_Steps: 1. Open Capture Vitals | 2. Set encounter datetime in the future | 3. Attempt to Save
Expected_Result: Future encounter datetime is rejected or warned; vitals cannot be recorded for a future time

Title: Verify BMI is not calculated when only weight is present
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; on the Capture Vitals form
Test_Steps: 1. Open Capture Vitals | 2. Enter Weight only, leave Height blank | 3. Save | 4. View dashboard
Expected_Result: No BMI is computed or a blank/NA BMI is shown; no divide-by-zero or erroneous value appears

Title: Verify vitals capture parity through the Resource Adapter Layer across backends
Preconditions: Adapter configured to route to OpenMRS and a second backend (e.g. HAPI FHIR); test patient exists in both
Test_Steps: 1. Capture identical vitals via the adapter for each backend | 2. Read back via the adapter | 3. Compare normalized output
Expected_Result: Both backends accept the vitals and the adapter returns equivalent normalized vital-sign data (LOINC/UCUM consistent)

Title: Verify a confirmation is shown and form clears after successful save
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; on the Capture Vitals form
Test_Steps: 1. Enter valid vitals | 2. Click Save | 3. Observe the UI response
Expected_Result: A success confirmation is displayed and the form returns to the dashboard or clears, ready for the next capture
